lead_profile_text: First-time STL setup from existing primary home. Self-contained basement studio, 10 Kingston Road, Oxford OX2 6EF. Own street-level access, kitchenette (hob, microwave, sink, no oven). No separate bedroom — competes with hotels/serviced accommodation. Residential mortgage with 90-day restriction on stays under 28 nights.
emotional_profile: Highly motivated — property nearly ready, numbers make sense. Initial scepticism about software figures resolved honestly on call. Analytical: raised tax, compliance, mortgage restriction in detail. Needs wife buy-in before signing.
delay_trigger_phrases: I'll have a chat with my wife about it — she obviously makes decisions
I just wanted to check it with my mortgage lender
green_light_trigger_phrases: Target live date approximately one month away

top_objections.text: Initial £73,620 gross figure flagged as suspicious — seemed too high for a studio
top_objections.zac_response: Acknowledged honestly in real time; revised range to £34k–£43k gross from Oxford comparables; corrected figures sent in follow-up email

top_objections.text: Mortgage 90-day restriction on stays under 28 nights
top_objections.zac_response: Highlighted that 28+ night bookings don't count toward cap; long-stay blend worth exploring with mortgage lender

top_objections.text: Short-let-only contract — wants to confirm terms before signing
top_objections.zac_response: Zac to confirm with partner by EOD 26 June 2026 whether Stayful can accommodate short-let-only basis
Midlands/Leeds HQ, local Oxford cleaning and maintenance teams in place

top_objections.text: Do laundry fees count towards the rental room allowance?
top_objections.zac_response: Suggested trading company structure — company pays lodger allowance of ~£7k/year tax-free, additional profits at corp tax rate

top_questions.text: Does the rental room allowance and laundry fee treatment work under a trading company structure?
top_questions.answer: Trading company suggested: company pays ~£7k/year lodger allowance tax-free, additional profits taxed at corp tax rate. Nick to confirm with accountant.

top_questions.text: Is PAT testing mandatory for STL?
top_questions.answer: Not mandatory for short-term lets. EPC valid ~5 years (fine since bought 1 year ago). Gas safety annual. STL insurance required.

top_questions.text: Can we sign on a short-let-only basis while I confirm mortgage restriction with lender?
top_questions.answer: Zac to confirm with partner by EOD whether short-let-only contract is possible.

top_questions.text: Do 28-night stays count toward the 90-day mortgage restriction cap?
top_questions.answer: No — stays of 28 nights or more do not count toward the 90-day cap. Worth exploring long-stay blend with mortgage lender.

what_moved_them: Trading company tax structure suggestion — visibly interested, hadn't thought of it
Long-stay workaround for 90-day mortgage restriction — 'I'd be open to that'
Zac's honesty in real time about the initial figures being wrong — built trust
Stayful covering everything on the management side
what_fell_flat: Initial £73k software figures flagged as suspicious by Zac himself on the call — trust risk, though recovered well

conversion_gaps.gap: Wife buy-in not established on call
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Frame the 17 July offer deadline with the July income opportunity (~£2,700 peak month) in follow-up email

conversion_gaps.gap: Short-let-only contract terms not yet confirmed by Zac's partner
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Confirm by EOD 26 June as promised; include confirmation in follow-up email
Reassure that 28-night stays don't count; this can be resolved in parallel with contract signing

offer_framing: 13% + VAT (discounted from 15%), expires 17 July 2026. Peak July income of ~£2,700 is material urgency lever. Frictionless setup (~£675 cost).
zac_close_phrase: Touch base in a couple of weeks
zac_income_intro: Revised Oxford comparable range: large double room nearby ~£34k gross, self-contained annex with parking ~£43k gross. Nick's studio falls between — corrected midpoint £40k Y2 gross / £21,600 net.
zac_reassurance_phrases: 28+ night bookings don't count toward the 90-day cap — worth exploring with your mortgage lender
PAT testing not mandatory for short-term lets
EPC is fine — bought a year ago, valid for ~5 more years
zac_analogies: Trading company structure: company pays Nick a lodger allowance of ~£7k/year tax-free, additional profits at corp tax rate
